Captures the transactional moment + the seller. Examples: Dunk purchasing the dragon-of-Pentos shield from Pate the Old; ship-passage purchases (when concretely named); merchant exchanges that are plot-significant. | Buyer → Seller | — |
342	| `BUILT` | Character physically built or oversaw the construction of a named structure (castle, tower, sept, wall, monument). Distinct from `FOUNDED` (scoped to organizations/houses/orders) and `OWNS` (steady state). Use when prose explicitly names the builder of a place. Examples: Brandon-the-Builder/The Wall, Brandon-the-Builder/Storm's End (legend), Lord-Triston-Hightower/Starry-Sept. | Builder → Structure (`place.location`) | — |
343	| `CAPTAIN_OF` | Character is captain (master/commander) of a named vessel. Target MUST be `object.artifact` (the vessel). Distinct from `COMMANDS` (military org) and `OWNS` (ownership state — captains may or may not own the vessel). Examples: Davos Seaworth/Black Betha, Victarion Greyjoy/Iron Victory, Asha Greyjoy/Black Wind, Salladhor Saan/Valyrian. | Captain → Vessel (`object.artifact`) | — |

Note specific role in `notes` if known ("first mate"). | Crew member → Vessel (`object.artifact`) | — |

346	### Identity & Disguise
347	
348	| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
349	|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
350	| `ALIAS_OF` | Known by another name. **Substitution test (canonical rule, S86 2026-06-08):** two strings are aliases iff substituting one for the other in any sentence about the entity does NOT change the truth value. "Wedding at the Twins" ↔ "Red Wedding" passes — same event. "Lord Walder calls for the bedding" ↔ "Red Wedding" FAILS — the bedding-call is a moment WITHIN the Red Wedding, not the whole event. Granularity differences are NOT aliases; emit `SUB_BEAT_OF` instead. The dashes-vs-spaces test catches surface variants only, NOT granularity. | Alias → True Identity | Alias, Aliases |
351	| `DISGUISED_AS` | Actively pretending to be someone/something else | Person → Disguise Identity | — |
352	| `SAME_AS` | Two references that resolve to the same entity (for cross-identity matching) | Symmetric | — |
353	| `IMPERSONATES` | Pretending to be a specific other person | Impersonator → Impersonated | — |
354	
355	### Magic & Supernatural
356	
357	> These edges capture relationships that involve magical, ritual, or supernatural agency — distinct from in-world physical relations. ASOIAF has rich magical systems (warging, R'hllor blood magic, Faceless-Men identity-transfer, weirwood-bonding, dragon-bonding, resurrection, curse-laying) that infobox extraction cannot reach. These types are *prose-derived only* — the wiki-infobox parser does not emit them.
358	
359	| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
360	|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
361	| `WARGS_INTO` | A warg / skinchanger actively occupies the consciousness of an animal or person — e.g., Bran into Summer / Hodor / the heart tree; Arya into Nymeria (dream-skinchange); Varamyr into his wolf / eagle / shadowcat; Orell into his eagle; Jon (low-key) into Ghost | Warg → Vessel | — |
362	| `BONDED_TO` | Static magical bond between two beings — broader and more permanent than `WARGS_INTO` (which is the active occupation moment). Covers dragon-rider bonds (Daenerys ↔ Drogon, etc.), warg-animal lifelong pairing (Bran ↔ Summer when not actively warging), weirwood-bond (Bran ↔ his three-eyed-crow / weirwood network) | Symmetric | — |
363	| `SACRIFICES` | Deliberate ritual or magical killing with supernatural/symbolic purpose — distinct from `KILLS` (combat) and `EXECUTES` (judicial). Mirri Maz Duur sacrificing Drogo's life-essence; Daenerys sacrificing her unborn child to magic the dragon eggs; Stannis (via Melisandre) sacrificing Edric Storm's leech-blood / Mance / Penny's brother / (theory-tier) Shireen; Craster sacrificing his sons to the Others | Sacrificer → Victim | — |
364	| `RESURRECTS` | Returns the dead to life via supernatural means — distinct from `HEALS` (medical), distinct from `KILLED_BY` (semantic reverse). Thoros of Myr resurrects Beric Dondarrion (multiple times); Beric resurrects Catelyn → Lady Stoneheart (ASOS Epilogue); Coldhands resurrected by unknown force (Children?); Patchface drowned-and-returned; Qyburn reanimates the Mountain; Red Priests broadly perform this (Thoros, Moqorro) — `HEALS` is for body-restoration, `RESURRECTS` is for death-reversal | Resurrector → Resurrected | — |
365	| `CURSES` | A character or magical force lays a curse — Mirri Maz Duur's "when the sun rises in the west" curse on Daenerys; Maggy the Frog's Valonqar prophecy-curse on Cersei; the Curse of Harrenhal (collective); Night's King lore | Curser → Cursed | — |
366	| `PRACTICES` | Character actively practices a named magical or ritual discipline. Target is `concept.magic` (or `concept.craft` for non-magical-but-named-discipline cases). Distinct from `WARGS_INTO` (active occupation moment), `BONDED_TO` (static pairing), `WORSHIPS` (religious devotion, not the magical practice itself), `CLERGY_OF` (religious office). Examples: Melisandre/shadow-binding, Mirri Maz Duur/maegi-blood-magic, Bran/greendreams, faceless-men/identity-transfer, Qyburn/necromancy. | Character → Magic discipline | — |
367

369	
370	| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
371	|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
372	| `CULTURE_OF` | Person belongs to a cultural group | Person → Culture | Culture, Race |
373	| `WORSHIPS` | Follows or serves a deity/religion | Person → Religion | Religion |
374	| `SACRED_TO` | Location or artifact is holy to a religion | Entity → Religion | — |
375	| `CLERGY_OF` | Serves as religious official | Person → Religion | — |
376	| `OFFICIATES` | Character performs the ritual / religious / ceremonial role at a named event (weddings, funerals, coronations, kingsmoots, namedays, knighting ceremonies). Distinct from `CLERGY_OF` (general clergy status, target = religion) and `ATTENDS` (guest/audience). Target is `event.*` or specific named ceremony node. Examples: Melisandre/wedding-of-sigorn-and-alys-karstark; the High Septon/coronation-of-tommen-i; Aeron Damphair/kingsmoot-of-299-ac. | Character → Event | — |
377	| `HONORED_AT` | Character is the **ceremonial honoree** — the recipient of a conferred honor at a named event (queen of love and beauty crowned at a tourney, a nameday honoree, the dubbed knight at a knighting). The reification-role complement on a ceremonial event hub: the **conferrer** uses `AGENT_IN` (e.g. Rhaegar laying the laurel), the **honoree** uses `HONORED_AT`. Retrospective: Ser Humfrey Hardyng "won a great melee at Maidenpool last year"; Ser Robyn Rhysling "lost his eye to a splinter... not five years earlier"; Dunk served at Highgarden "before you were ever born."
- **chapter_summary:** On the first day of the Ashford tourney, Dunk carries the boy Egg on his shoulders among the smallfolk to watch the jousting, in which the five maid's champions (the two Ashford brothers, Leo Tyrell, Humfrey Hardyng, and Prince Valarr) defend against a stream of challengers. Prince Aerion Brightflame deliberately drives his lance into Ser Humfrey Hardyng's horse, crushing Humfrey's leg. That evening Raymun Fossoway invites Dunk to wine in his cousin Ser Steffon's pavilion, where Dunk learns of Prince Maekar's absent sons; Egg then bursts in reporting that Aerion is assaulting the puppet-girl Tanselle. Dunk beats Aerion, is seized by Aerion's men-at-arms, and is freed only when Egg reveals himself to be Aerion's brother and Prince Maekar's son.

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Anxious before the day (belly "hard as a rock"); humbled listening to Egg's knowledge; sickened by the killing of Humfrey's horse; then enraged and horrified at Aerion's assault on Tanselle; braced for death when captured ("he means to kill me... there was hardly even time to be afraid").
- **Primary preoccupation:** Whether he can win at least the first challenge on the morrow, and which champion he could beat; he studies Valarr closely and concludes "I would have a chance against him," while recognizing a hedge knight cannot challenge a prince.
- **Key decisions made:** To accept Raymun's hospitality despite discomfort; to run to Tanselle's aid and beat Aerion, wrenching free of Raymun's restraining hand ("he could not listen").
- **Self-deception flags:** Insists aloud "I saw a knight green as summer grass lose control of his lance" though "it had looked that way to him as well" that Aerion meant to kill the horse; frames his social discomfort among lords as inability to "return" hospitality.

- **book:** THK

- **pov_character:** Dunk
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Ashford Meadow (the tourney grounds / the lists)
- **location_secondary:** the viewing stand, merchants' row, the Fossoway pavilion, the puppeteer's stall

- **time_markers:** "The first day of the tourney dawned bright and clear"; morning; "For the rest of the morning and well into the afternoon"; "Late in the day"; "the sun was low in the west"; "the shadows of evening crept across the meadow"; "a hundred torches were lit"; night. Retrospective: Ser Humfrey Hardyng "won a great melee at Maidenpool last year"; Ser Robyn Rhysling "lost his eye to a splinter... not five years earlier"; Dunk served at Highgarden "before you were ever born."
- **chapter_summary:** On the first day of the Ashford tourney, Dunk carries the boy Egg on his shoulders among the smallfolk to watch the jousting, in which the five maid's champions (the two Ashford brothers, Leo Tyrell, Humfrey Hardyng, and Prince Valarr) defend against a stream of challengers. Prince Aerion Brightflame deliberately drives his lance into Ser Humfrey Hardyng's horse, crushing Humfrey's leg. That evening Raymun Fossoway invites Dunk to wine in his cousin Ser Steffon's pavilion, where Dunk learns of Prince Maekar's absent sons; Egg then bursts in reporting that Aerion is assaulting the puppet-girl Tanselle. Dunk beats Aerion, is seized by Aerion's men-at-arms, and is freed only when Egg reveals himself to be Aerion's brother and Prince Maekar's son.
- **unit_part:** 4 of 7

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Bright and clear at dawn; gusty winds that snapped and tugged at the pennons; "the wind whipping Ser Robyn's hair."
- **Season indicators:** A knight described as "green as summer grass"; "the morning air."
- **Time of day:** Dawn, morning, afternoon, late day, sunset, evening/night.
- **Lighting:** Sunlight flashing golden off Baelor's clasp and coronet; a hundred torches lit along the merchant's row at evening; a brazier of coals lighting the Fossoway pavilion interior; torch used to set puppets afire.
- **Sounds:** A thousand voices screaming and shouting; forty iron-shod hooves pounding; the rending crash of wood and steel; roar of approval; steel singing on steel; the almost-human screams of the dying horse; Tanselle's scream; a sprightly air on pipes and drums; heralds booming out names; trumpet fanfares.
- **Smells:** (none stated)
- **Notable sensory details:** Dunk feeling the earth tremble beneath his feet; the goat basted with honey and herbs over a cookfire; the air in the Fossoway pavilion "pleasantly warm" from the brazier; shards of broken lances flying "like wooden knives"; bright blood gouting from the speared horse.

# THK — Dunk I

Retrospective: Ser Humfrey Hardyng "won a great melee at Maidenpool last year"; Ser Robyn Rhysling "lost his eye to a splinter... not five years earlier"; Dunk served at Highgarden "before you were ever born."
- **chapter_summary:** On the first day of the Ashford tourney, Dunk carries the boy Egg on his shoulders among the smallfolk to watch the jousting, in which the five maid's champions (the two Ashford brothers, Leo Tyrell, Humfrey Hardyng, and Prince Valarr) defend against a stream of challengers. Prince Aerion Brightflame deliberately drives his lance into Ser Humfrey Hardyng's horse, crushing Humfrey's leg. That evening Raymun Fossoway invites Dunk to wine in his cousin Ser Steffon's pavilion, where Dunk learns of Prince Maekar's absent sons; Egg then bursts in reporting that Aerion is assaulting the puppet-girl Tanselle. Dunk beats Aerion, is seized by Aerion's men-at-arms, and is freed only when Egg reveals himself to be Aerion's brother and Prince Maekar's son.

- **Lighting:** Sunlight flashing golden off Baelor's clasp and coronet; a hundred torches lit along the merchant's row at evening; a brazier of coals lighting the Fossoway pavilion interior; torch used to set puppets afire.

- **Smells:** (none stated)

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Anxious before the day (belly "hard as a rock"); humbled listening to Egg's knowledge; sickened by the killing of Humfrey's horse; then enraged and horrified at Aerion's assault on Tanselle; braced for death when captured ("he means to kill me... there was hardly even time to be afraid").
- **Primary preoccupation:** Whether he can win at least the first challenge on the morrow, and which champion he could beat; he studies Valarr closely and concludes "I would have a chance against him," while recognizing a hedge knight cannot challenge a prince.
- **Key decisions made:** To accept Raymun's hospitality despite discomfort; to run to Tanselle's aid and beat Aerion, wrenching free of Raymun's restraining hand ("he could not listen").
- **Self-deception flags:** Insists aloud "I saw a knight green as summer grass lose control of his lance" though "it had looked that way to him as well" that Aerion meant to kill the horse; frames his social discomfort among lords as inability to "return" hospitality.
